english: the letter t aspirated, like the English t in 'tea'.
1. num. fig.: ten. - 2. every thing, all, total Sch. (?).
a certain star, t'a-skár-zla-ba (tha skar zla ba) a month, prob. = वैशाख (April-May); t'a-skár-gyi bu (tha skar gyi bu) अश्विनी twin half-gods.
vulg. t'i-gu (thi gu), 1. a short cord or rope. - 2. string, twine, for making garlands Stg.; a bell-rope Dzl.

originally t'ag-grú (thag gru) Pth., extension, width, breadth, odzam-bu-gliṅ-gi t'a-grú kún-la ('dzam bu gling gi tha gru kun la) Glr. in the whole extent of Dzambuling; t'a-grú čé-ba (tha gru che ba) Pth. extensive.

the last month of a season (v. dus (dus)), e.g. dpyíd-zla t'a-čúṅ (dpyid zla tha chung) the last month of spring, opp. to rá-ba (ra ba), (and obriṅ - po ('bring po)); the youngest of three or more sons, opp. to rab (rab) (and obriṅ-po ('bring po) the middle one).

1. appellation, žes t'a-snyád-du grags (zhes tha snyad du grags) so it is called Wdn.; Tar. 96, 13; 178, 3; Was. (296): supposition; condition, t'a-snyád-pai bdén-pa (tha snyad pai bden pa) conditional truth. - 3. Schr.: etymology, Cs. only: part of grammar; so frq. used by grammarians, e.g. tsig daṅ t'a-snyád slób-pa (tshig dang tha snyad slob pa) to learn spelling and etymology. - 4. In col. language I heard it used only for talking or disputing in a conceited, foolish manner, so also in Mil. - Lex. in conformity with each of these significations = व्यवहार, from व्यवहृ to distinguish, to name; to dispute. - t'a-snyád-γčíg-pa (tha snyad gcig pa) n. of a school, of a system or doctrine Tar.; t'a-snyád-grúb-pa (tha snyad grub pa) n. of a literary work.
different, various, sundry, gen. opp to γčíg (gcig) or γčíg-pa (gcig pa); dgós-pa t'a-dád-pa (dgos pa tha dad pa) the various wants of a man Dzl.; t'a-mi-dád-pa (tha mi dad pa) alike, equal.

even, so much as, up to, t'á-na srog-čágs gróg-sbur yan-čád (tha na srog chags grog sbur yan chad) even the smallest insect Stg.; t'á-na yig-obru re-ré yan-čád (tha na yig 'bru re re yan chad) even every single letter Thgy.; at the close of an enumeration: finally also Ld.-Glr. Schl. 20, 6.

the last of several things, with respect to number, time, rank, the lowest, meanest, most inferior, often opp. to rab (rab) and obriṅ ('bring), and also to kyád-par-čan (khyad par can); it appears somewhat singular, that γdan-zán-gyi t'á-ma (gdan zan gyi tha ma) signifies a cat, and odab-čágs-kyi t'á-ma ('dab chags kyi tha ma) a hen Glr.; dús-kyi t'á-ma-la (dus kyi tha ma la) in the last times Glr., prob. also alluding to the general decline taking place towards the end of the Kalpa; sometimes it is to be translated: in the last place, finally, at last Glr., like t'á-mar (tha mar) Dzl. 2, 11; last = parting (parting-cup, parting-kiss); for the last time: γnyén-gyi t'á-mas bskor (gnyen gyi tha mas bskor) he sees his relations for the last time around him, zás-kyi t'á-ma za (zas kyi tha ma za) he eats for the last time Thgy.; t'á-ma-la (tha ma la) c. genit. at the end of, after. - oprád-pai t'á-ma ni obral, gsón-pai t'á-ma ni čí-ba yin ('phrad pai tha ma ni 'bral, gson pai tha ma ni chi ba yin) the end of every meeting is parting, the end of every living is dying.
Cs., vulg. W. *t'á-mag* (*tha mag*), tobacco, otuṅ-ba ('thung ba), W. resp. *dóṅ-če* (*dong ce*) to smoke (tobacco).
